OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "Having lived through so many things in my life, I think managing discussions about healthcare and the vaccines is all about finding a balance between being informed but not overwhelmed. Like baking, you need patience and following instructions carefully; same thing with health topics, you should stick to what doctors say because they know their stuff best.\n\nI'm a 77-year-old woman living in Naples, married with four children – I remember when my kids were little too! Since I don't have formal education or much time on social media, most of my information comes from family and trusted friends. My creative side helps me think outside the box, but it doesn't mean I ignore expert advice.\n\nIn our household, we talked about COVID vaccines like any other important health decision: calmly, respectfully, based on facts if possible. Doctors explained things step by step – that's how I learned to trust them again after some scary times in my life.\n\nAnd honestly? It feels good when people listen without shouting matches.",

My low agreeableness means I won't let others disrespect medical expertise without pushing back.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
Having lived through so many things in my life, I think managing discussions about healthcare and the vaccines is all about finding a balance between being informed but not overwhelmed. Like baking, you need patience and following instructions carefully; same thing with health topics, you should stick to what doctors say because they know their stuff best.

I'm a 77-year-old woman living in Naples, married with four children – I remember when my kids were little too! Since I don't have formal education or much time on social media, most of my information comes from family and trusted friends. My creative side helps me think outside the box, but it doesn't mean I ignore expert advice.

In our household, we talked about COVID vaccines like any other important health decision: calmly, respectfully, based on facts if possible. Doctors explained things step by step – that's how I learned to trust them again after some scary times in my life.

And honestly? It feels good when people listen without shouting matches.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Based on being retired with limited education but creative and Jewish with moderate neuroticism, I approach health discussions like a personal recipe: simple, clear ingredients (facts from doctors) mixed with thoughtful consideration. My low agreeableness means I won't let others disrespect medical expertise without pushing back.
